description: Strengthen landing page with proof-of-work section, enhanced embedded model messaging, geographic diversity map, horizontal scroll testimonials, and supply-side community messaging.

userStories.title: Create ProofOfWork section component
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to see what types of projects Claru has delivered so that I trust they can handle my data needs.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/components/sections/ProofOfWork.tsx
Section label in monospace: // WHAT WE'VE SHIPPED
Headline: Built for labs training at the frontier.
Grid of 5 project type cards, each with: Lucide icon, project type name, concrete metric, 1-line description
Card 1: Egocentric Video Capture — 12,000+ hours collected across 14 countries
Card 2: Manipulation Trajectory Data — 900 hours of action breakdowns delivered in 3 weeks
Card 3: Game Environment Capture — Proprietary platform, 50+ game titles, on-demand volume
Card 4: Multi-Modal Annotation — Frame-level video, spatial, RLHF with real-time QA loops
Card 5: Synthetic Data Pipelines — Unreal and Omniverse environments, sim-to-real validated
Bottom link text: Explore our full data catalog pointing to #contact
Uses whileInView fade-in with staggered card reveals (index * 0.1s delay)
Uses TextScramble on card title hover
Cards use --bg-card background, --border-subtle border, 1rem radius, hover lift and border glow
Responsive: 1-col mobile, 2-col tablet (md:), 3-col desktop (lg:) with last 2 spanning below
npm run build succeeds
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Create animated geographic diversity map component
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to see a world map showing Claru's global contributor network so that I understand the geographic diversity of their data.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/components/ui/GlobeMap.tsx
Renders an animated flat world map using inline SVG continent outlines at very low opacity (5-8%)

Glowing dots in --accent-primary color at approximate locations for: Philippines, India, Pakistan, US (2 dots east/west), Canada, Brazil, Mexico, UK, Germany, Japan, South Korea, Australia, Kenya, South Africa
Dots pulse/appear with staggered Framer Motion whileInView animation
Small monospace region labels visible: NORTH AMERICA, SOUTH AMERICA, EUROPE, AFRICA, SOUTH ASIA, EAST ASIA, OCEANIA
Labels styled as font-mono text-xs text-[var(--text-muted)] uppercase tracking-wider
Stats line below map: Contributors across 14+ countries — styled in accent-secondary
Dark background matching --bg-primary, no heavy map libraries (no Mapbox, Leaflet)
Responsive: scales down on mobile, reduce label count on small screens
Lightweight: pure SVG paths for continent outlines, dots are SVG circles with CSS animation
npm run build succeeds
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Create GlobalReach section wrapper and integrate map into page
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to wrap the GlobeMap in a section and place it between TwoPaths and ProofOfWork on the main page.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/components/sections/GlobalReach.tsx as thin section wrapper
Section label: // GLOBAL REACH
Headline: Data from everywhere your model will deploy.
Subtext: Your model needs to generalize. Your data should too.
Contains the GlobeMap component
Section uses FadeIn or whileInView animation on scroll
In src/app/page.tsx add dynamic import for GlobalReach with ssr: false and SectionSkeleton loading
In src/app/page.tsx add dynamic import for ProofOfWork with ssr: false and SectionSkeleton loading
Page render order becomes: Hero, ProblemAgitation, SectionBridge, Origin, TwoPaths, GlobalReach, ProofOfWork, Testimonials, FinalCTA, AnimatedLogo
npm run build succeeds
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill — scroll through full page, confirm section ordering

userStories.title: Enhance TwoPaths with embedded model and proprietary tooling
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to understand that Claru has proprietary tools and an embedded working model so that I see them as more than a generic vendor.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/app/components/sections/TwoPaths.tsx update Training Data card features to include: Proprietary video game capture platform, Proprietary labeling and QA platform (add to or replace existing items, keep 4-5 total)

Update Expert Labeling card features to include: Embedded in your Slack — real-time feedback not batch cycles, Proprietary VLM for machine-assisted captioning and labeling (add to or replace existing items, keep 4-5 total)
Add a speed/flexibility callout below the two cards: centered text reading First samples in 48 hours. Requirements change mid-project — we adapt in hours, not weeks.
Callout styled as font-mono text-sm text-[var(--accent-secondary)] with whileInView fade animation
Existing card layout, hover effects, and animations remain unchanged
npm run build succeeds
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill — confirm cards look balanced, no text overflow

userStories.title: Convert Testimonials to horizontal scroll
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to see testimonials in a compact horizontal scroll so that they are present but do not dominate the page.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/app/components/sections/Testimonials.tsx keep the 3 metric cards at the top (8wk, 100% embedded, full pipeline) unchanged
Convert the 4 testimonial cards from 2x2 grid to a horizontal scrolling row
Scroll container uses overflow-x: auto with scroll-snap-type: x mandatory and scroll-snap-align: start on each card
Each card has fixed width ~320px desktop, ~280px mobile with flex-shrink-0
Show partial next card at right edge to indicate scrollability (peek effect via padding)
Add subtle fade gradient at right edge of scroll container to hint at more content
Remove parallax blur circles background effect (simplify background)
Reduce section vertical padding from default section class to py-16 md:py-20 (roughly 5rem)
All 4 testimonials remain accessible via horizontal scroll
Works on mobile (touch scroll) and desktop (mouse/trackpad scroll)
npm run build succeeds
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill — test scroll on desktop and mobile viewport sizes

userStories.title: Add community messaging to work-with-us page
userStories.description: As a potential contributor visiting /work-with-us, I want to see that Claru values its community so that I feel confident joining.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/app/work-with-us/page.tsx add a callout section after the hero area and before the bento grid
Headline: Join a team, not a task queue.
Body text: We answer every message. We know our contributors by name. When you join Claru, you are part of a team building the data that trains the world's most advanced AI.
3 small inline stats: 500+ active contributors, Avg response time under 2 hours, Projects across 14+ countries
Styled as a dark card with --bg-card background, --border-subtle border, accent border on hover
Section label in monospace: // OUR COMMUNITY
Uses whileInView fade animation matching existing page patterns
Does not break existing bento grid layout below it
npm run build succeeds
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Playwright E2E Test — Landing page sections and scroll
userStories.description: As QA, I need an end-to-end test verifying all landing page sections render correctly after the revisions.
acceptanceCriteria: Create tests/e2e/landing-page-revisions.spec.ts
Test: navigate to localhost:3000 and wait for page load
Test: scroll through entire page
Test: assert ProofOfWork section is visible and contains 5 project cards
Test: assert GlobalReach section is visible and contains SVG map element
Test: assert Testimonials section has horizontal scroll container
Test: assert TwoPaths section contains text Embedded in your Slack
Test: assert TwoPaths section contains text First samples in 48 hours
Test: assert Book a Call CTA is present in at least 2 locations
Test: navigate to localhost:3000/work-with-us
Test: assert community callout with Join a team, not a task queue text is visible
Typecheck passes
